packagingTargets.name: source-tree-default
packagingTargets.notes: Builds and tests the engine and sample games from the source tree.

packagingTargets.name: desktop-gui
packagingTargets.notes: Builds the deferred editor-core verification lane while the visible editor shell is parked until a first-party native desktop editor host lands; it does not enable SDL3 or Dear ImGui.

packagingTargets.name: desktop-game-runtime
packagingTargets.notes: Builds the optional editor-independent desktop game runtime lane through the desktop-runtime preset with Windows-native host evidence only: Win32 platform and WASAPI tests, runtime host tests including DiagnosticsRecorder-backed frame profiles and renderer counter capture, MK_runtime_host_win32 public/API tests, finite sample_desktop_runtime_shell --smoke, DXC-available build-output DXIL artifact smoke paths, DXC SPIR-V CodeGen-available build-output SPIR-V artifact smoke paths, the Win32 config-only sample_generated_desktop_runtime_package scaffold proof, the Win32 cooked-scene scaffold proof with --require-scene-package and native NullRenderer fallback, the Win32 sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package source-tree/package proof, and the DXC-available sample_generated_desktop_runtime_material_shader_package shader-artifact proof. The previously SDL3-backed non-shell and generated 3D samples are no longer active registered desktop-runtime package targets until they are ported to the first-party native host or removed.

packagingTargets.name: desktop-runtime-release
packagingTargets.notes: Builds the desktop-runtime-release preset, cleans the desktop runtime install prefix, installs a selected registered runtime game executable plus selected package files derived from target metadata, selected D3D12 DXIL and explicitly requested Vulkan SPIR-V shader artifacts when declared, rejects SDL3.dll in installed and archived Windows runtime artifacts, writes share/Mirakanai/desktop-runtime-games.json package metadata with selectedPackageTarget, gameManifest paths, packageFiles, d3d12ShaderArtifacts, and vulkanShaderArtifacts, validates the installed SDK consumer, runs the installed selected game smoke from metadata, verifies the selected installed game manifest, package files, required shader artifacts, and selected presentation/scene/package status fields, and creates a CPack ZIP without requiring Dear ImGui or MK_editor. The default target is sample_desktop_runtime_shell; selected Win32 package targets currently include sample_generated_desktop_runtime_package, sample_generated_desktop_runtime_cooked_scene_package, sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package, and sample_generated_desktop_runtime_material_shader_package. Previously SDL3-backed non-shell and generated 3D targets are not active package targets in this lane.

packagingTargets.name: asset-importers
packagingTargets.notes: Builds MK_tools with optional libspng, fastgltf, and miniaudio importer adapters and runs importer-capable tests.

packagingTargets.name: windows-cpack-zip
packagingTargets.notes: Builds Release, runs Release tests, installs the SDK layout, validates examples/installed_consumer against the installed mirakana:: package, and creates a CPack ZIP with package metadata.

packagingTargets.name: android-gameactivity
packagingTargets.notes: Android Gradle Plugin 9.1.0, Gradle 9.3.1, JDK 17, Android SDK Platform 36.1, Build Tools 36.0.0, Android SDK CMake 4.1.2, NDK CMake, Prefab, AndroidX AppCompat 1.7.1, AndroidX Core 1.18.0, GameActivity 3.0.4, NDK Vulkan loader linkage, private ANativeWindow/VK_KHR_android_surface creation bridge, private low-latency AAudio float32 output adapter, generated asset packaging, host-validated Debug/Release APK output, Release signing verification with non-repository upload keys, upload certificate export, deterministic ANDROID_AVD_HOME normalization, AVD readiness diagnostics, and emulator install/launch smoke coverage exist; generated game manifests are validated and packaged before package build attempts, and pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/check-mobile-packaging.ps1 reports signing/toolchain/AVD/device blockers explicitly with stable kind slugs.

packagingTargets.name: ios-metal
packagingTargets.notes: Apple CMake/Xcode iOS bundle template exists with UIKit/Metal framework linkage, Metal-backed root view setup, sandbox save/cache/shared storage root mapping, game manifest resource packaging, bundle identifier inputs, simulator/device SDK selection, simulator signing suppression, Xcode signing inputs, iOS Simulator install/launch smoke scripting through pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/smoke-ios-package.ps1, and explicit full-Xcode/iOS-SDK/iOS-runtime blockers with stable kind slugs from pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/check-mobile-packaging.ps1; actual host-validated simulator/device results and backend-neutral Metal presentation remain Apple-host gated.
